COCOS, or Boscawen's Island, an island of the S Pacific, in the Friendly group, in S lat. 16° 5', and W long. 174° 15'. It rises in the form of a cone, and is about 3 m. in diameter. It was discovered in 1616 by Le Maire.
COCOS, or MARQUEEN ISLANDS, a group of the South Pacific, to the N of the Salomon islands, in S lat. 4° 30', and E long. 156° 35'. They were discovered in 1795.
COCOS (Point), a headland on the coast of New Grenada, in the dep. of Ystmo, on the E side of Escribanos harbour, in N lat. 9° 34', W long. 77° 12'.

COCOULO, a river of Central Africa, in the Foulah prov. of Timbi. Where Caillie crossed it between Bouma and Popoco, it was from 40 to 45 paces wide, flowing rapidly over a bed of granite from NNE to SSW, between high mountains covered with large trees. At a little distance from the point where he forded it, it falls over a precipice, forming a cataract 60 ft. high.
COCUMONT, a commune and town of France, in the dep. of the Lot-et-Garonne, cant. of Meilhan, 13 m. SW of Marmande. Pop. 1,676.
COCUZZO, or CERVARO, a mountain of Naples, in the chain of the Apennines, in the prov. of Calabria Citra, on the confines of the districts of Cosenza and Paola, 8 m. SW of Cosenza.

COD (CAPE), the extreme point of Barnstable peninsula, in the state of Massachusetts, U. S., in N lat. 42° 4', W long. 70° 4'. [Raper.] The peninsula defines the S portion of a large bay, known as Massachusetts bay, which extends N to Cape Anne. The S portion of this bay is generally known as Cape Cod bay or Barnstable bay. In the 21st vol. of the Foreign Quarterly Review, in an article on the discovery of America by the Northmen, before Columbus, it is maintained that Cape C. was the Kialarnes of Thorwald, who reached this point in 1004. CODAGA, CADAJA, or CADAXA, the most easterly of the eight arms of the Hyapura or Japura, in the prov. of Para, Brazil. It issues from the Hyapura, 15 m. SE of S. Antonio-de-Marapi, runs ESE, communicates by several branches with the Amazon, and with the Rio Negro, by Lake Atinieni and the Rio Jaumuhi, and terminates in the Amazon at Asanaquane, after a course of nearly 300 m.
CODDENHAM, a parish of Suffolk, 31 m. ESE of Needham-Market. Area 1,540 acres. Pop. 924.

CODDINGTON, a parish and township of Cheshire, 6 m. NNW of Malpas. Area 2,640 acres. Pop. 324. Area of township, 1,220 acres. Pop. 109. -Also a parish of Herefordshire, 33 m. N of Ledbury. Area 1,510 acres. Pop. 158.—Also a parish of Nottinghamshire, 2½ m. ENE of Newark. Area 1,850 acres. Pop. 436.
CODERA (CAPE), a headland of Venezuela, in the gov. and 50 m. E of Caracas, in N lat. 10° 35' 54", on the E side of Puerto-Corsarios bay.
CODESAL, a town of Spain, in Leon, in the prov. and 30 m. NW of Zamora, partido and 16 m. ESE of La Puebla de Sanaria, near the r. bank of the Tera.
CODEVILLA, a village of Piedmont, dio. of Alexandria, mand. of Casteggio, prov. and 4 m. SE of Voghera. Pop. 1,037.
CODFORD (St. Mary), a parish of Wilts, 4 m. ESE of Heytesbury, on the SE bank of the Wiley. Area 2,150 acres. Pop. 180.
CODFORD (St. Peter), a parish of Wilts, 5 m. SE of Heytesbury. Area 1,770 acres. Pop. 394. II. COE
CODICOTE, a parish of Hertfordshire, 2 m. NNW of Welwyn. Area 2,580 acres. Pop. 906.
CODNOR-WITH-LOSCOW, a township in the p. of Heanor, in Derbyshire, 4 m. SSE of Alfreton. Pop. 2,007.
CODNOR-CASTLE, a village and liberty in the co. of Derby, 5½ m. ENE of Belper. Area 1,320 acres. Pop. 815.

CODOGNO, a town of Venetian Lombardy, in the gov. of Milan, deleg. of Lodi-et-Crema, 15 m. E of Lodi, near the confluence of the Adda and Po. Pop. 8,000. It has an extensive trade in the so-called Parmesan cheese, which is produced in a district about 20 m. wide from Pavia to Milan and Lodi, and 40 m. in length from Abbiategrasso, by the Ticino, to the town of C. The finer qualities of this cheese are not exported until after two or three years' seasoning in the warehouses here and at Corsico.

CODORUS, a village in York co., in the state of Pennsylvania, U. S., 37 m. S of Harrisburg, on a stream of the same name which, rising in Frederick co. in Maryland, enters the Susquehanna near New Holland in Pennsylvania.
CODROIPO, a town of Venetian Lombardy, in the gov. of Venice, deleg. and 14 m. SW of Udino. Pop. 3,000.
CODRONGIANUS, a village of Sardinia, in the prov. and 8 m. SE of Sassari. It is built on the sides of a gorge, with narrow, crooked, and steep streets. Pop. 1,464.
COD ROY, a low flat green island, 2 m. in circuit, 2 m. S of Cape Anguille, on the W coast of Newfoundland. It forms with the mainland a small snug bar-harbour for vessels of 10 or 12 ft. draught.
CODSALL, a parish and village in Staffordshire, 5 m. NW of Wolverhampton. Area 2,580 acres. Pop. 1,096.
CODUGU. See CURG.
